cities.name: Puducherry
cities.overview: Puducherry (formerly Pondicherry) is a charming coastal town that still carries the elegant French colonial legacy. Known for its tree-lined boulevards, pastel-coloured buildings, serene beaches, and spiritual aura, it offers a unique Indo-French cultural experience.
cities.history: Puducherry was a French colony from 1674 until 1954. The French influence is visible in its architecture, cuisine, and street names. It became a Union Territory of India in 1962. Sri Aurobindo and The Mother established the famous Ashram here, making it a global spiritual centre.

nearest_railhead.name: Puducherry Railway Station
nearest_railhead.note: Limited trains; Chennai is major hub

places.name: Promenade Beach
places.description: The iconic 1.5-km long beachfront promenade lined with colonial buildings, statues, and the famous Gandhi Memorial. Perfect for evening walks and people-watching.
places.tips: Best at sunset when the French Quarter lights up. Street food stalls serve delicious crepes and local snacks. Vehicle-free on weekends.

places.name: French Quarter (White Town)
places.description: The charming heritage area with pastel-coloured French colonial villas, boutique cafes, art galleries, and quiet cobbled lanes.
places.tips: Rent a bicycle or walk the streets. Many heritage homes are now boutique hotels and cafes. Best explored in the morning.

places.name: Sri Aurobindo Ashram
places.description: The world-famous ashram founded by Sri Aurobindo and The Mother. A serene spiritual centre with beautiful gardens and meditation halls.
places.tips: Maintain silence inside. Visitors can meditate in the courtyard. The ashram bookstore has excellent spiritual literature.

places.name: Paradise Beach (Chunnambar)
places.description: A pristine beach accessible only by boat across the backwaters. White sand, palm groves, and calm waters make it ideal for relaxation.
places.tips: Take the ferry from Chunnambar boat house. Carry water and snacks as facilities are limited. Best visited mid-week.

places.name: Auroville (nearby)
places.description: The international township founded in 1968 as a universal town where people live in peace and harmony beyond nationality and religion.
places.tips: Visit the Matrimandir (golden sphere) for meditation (prior booking required). Respect the silence and dress code.

cities.name: Auroville
cities.overview: Auroville is an experimental universal township dedicated to human unity and harmonious living. It attracts seekers from around the world with its iconic Matrimandir and sustainable lifestyle.
cities.history: Founded in 1968 by The Mother with the vision of Sri Aurobindo. Today it is home to people from over 50 nations living in harmony.

places.name: Matrimandir
places.description: The iconic golden spherical meditation chamber at the heart of Auroville. A symbol of the Divine Consciousness.
places.tips: Prior booking is mandatory for inner chamber meditation. Maintain complete silence. The surrounding gardens are beautiful.

cities.name: Karaikal
cities.overview: Karaikal is a charming French colonial enclave with beautiful beaches, temples, and a relaxed pace of life.
cities.history: Like Puducherry, Karaikal was under French rule until 1954 and retains strong French architectural and cultural influences.

places.name: Karaikal Beach
places.description: A long, clean beach with golden sand and calm waters, perfect for walks and family picnics.
places.tips: Best in the evening. Local seafood stalls serve fresh catch. Safe for swimming in designated areas.

cities.name: Mahe
cities.overview: Mahe is a picturesque French enclave on the Malabar coast, known for its riverside charm, colonial buildings, and serene beaches.
cities.history: Mahe remained a French possession until 1954 and still retains its French character and architecture.

places.name: Mahe Riverfront
places.description: Scenic promenade along the Mahe River with colonial buildings and beautiful sunset views.
places.tips: Perfect for evening walks. Local cafes serve excellent coffee and snacks.

cities.name: Yanam
cities.overview: Yanam is a quiet French enclave on the Godavari delta, known for its colonial charm, beaches, and backwaters.
cities.history: Yanam was a French colony until 1954 and retains its distinct French-influenced culture and architecture.

places.name: Yanam Beach
places.description: A peaceful, less-crowded beach with golden sand and calm waters along the Godavari delta.
places.tips: Great for relaxed walks and photography. Local seafood is excellent at beachside stalls.

cities.name: Paradise Beach
cities.overview: Paradise Beach (Chunnambar) is a secluded beach accessible only by boat, offering pristine white sand, palm groves, and a tranquil lagoon.
cities.history: Part of the backwater system near Puducherry, it has become one of the most popular day-trip destinations.

places.name: Paradise Beach
places.description: A secluded white-sand beach reachable only by ferry, surrounded by backwaters and coconut palms.
places.tips: Take the ferry from Chunnambar. Ideal for swimming and picnics. Carry your own snacks and water.

cities.name: Serenity Beach
cities.overview: Serenity Beach is a calm and less crowded beach north of Puducherry town, perfect for those seeking peace and natural beauty.
cities.history: Part of the growing beach tourism circuit around Puducherry.

places.name: Serenity Beach
places.description: A quiet, clean beach with soft sand and gentle waves, ideal for long walks and sunbathing.
places.tips: Less crowded than Promenade Beach. Great for sunrise walks. Local cafes serve fresh seafood.
